ability word: An ability word (like landfall or metalcraft, always italicized and lowercase) has no fixed rules meaning of its own; it just labels a card's own written ability text.
activated ability: An activated ability can be used any time its controller has priority by paying its cost, written as 'cost: effect'.
adapt: Adapt N puts N +1/+1 counters on a creature, but only if it has no counters on it already.
adventure: An Adventure card has an alternative spell (the Adventure) that can be cast from hand; after it resolves, the card is exiled and can later be cast as itself from exile.
afflict: Afflict N means that whenever a creature with afflict becomes blocked, its controller deals N damage to the blocking player.
afterlife: Afterlife N means that when a creature with afterlife dies, create N 1/1 white and black Spirit creature tokens with flying.
amass: Amass N means you put N +1/+1 counters on an Army creature you control, creating a 0/0 black Army token first if you don't control one.
amplify: Amplify N means as a creature with amplify enters, its controller may reveal cards of the same creature type from hand, putting N +1/+1 counters on it for each card revealed.
annihilator: Annihilator N means whenever a creature with annihilator attacks, defending player sacrifices N permanents.
attack: To attack, an untapped creature without summoning sickness is declared as an attacker during the declare attackers step, tapping it (unless it has vigilance).
aura: Aura is an enchantment subtype that attaches to a permanent or player when it enters, via its enchant keyword, granting an effect while attached.
banding: Creatures with banding (or blocked by one) let their controller choose damage assignment order and grouping in that combat.
battle cry: Whenever a creature with battle cry attacks, each other attacking creature gets +1/+0 until end of turn.
block: To block, a defending creature is declared to intercept one attacking creature during the declare blockers step, preventing that attacker from dealing combat damage to the player unless it has trample.
bloodthirst: Bloodthirst N means a creature enters with N +1/+1 counters on it if an opponent was dealt damage this turn.
bushido: Bushido N gives a creature +N/+N until end of turn whenever it blocks or becomes blocked.
cascade: When you cast a spell with cascade, exile cards from your library until you exile a nonland card costing less; you may cast it free, then put the rest on the bottom in a random order.
changeling: A creature with changeling is every creature type, always.
color identity: A card's color identity is the set of colors of any mana symbols in its mana cost or rules text, used for Commander deckbuilding legality.
combat damage: Combat damage is dealt simultaneously by all attacking and blocking creatures during the combat damage step (or in two steps if any have first/double strike).
commander: In the Commander format, each player's deck is built around a legendary creature (their commander) that starts in a special command zone.
commander damage: Commander damage is combat damage dealt by a commander, tracked separately; a player who has taken 21 or more from a single commander loses the game.
conspire: A spell with conspire can be cast a second time for free by tapping two untapped creatures that share a color with it.
convoke: A spell with convoke can be paid for in part by tapping untapped creatures you control instead of mana, each contributing one mana of its color (or one generic).
counter (a spell or ability): To counter a spell or ability means to cancel it, removing it from the stack without any of its effects happening (though costs already paid stay paid).
counter (marker): A counter is a marker placed on a permanent or player (e.g. +1/+1 counter, poison counter) that changes its characteristics or tracks a value.
cycling: A card with cycling can be discarded from hand by paying its cycling cost to draw a new card.
dash: A creature with dash may be cast for its dash cost, gaining haste, and returned to its owner's hand at end of turn.
deathtouch: Any amount of damage dealt by a source with deathtouch is enough to destroy the creature it damages.
defender: A creature with defender can't attack.
delve: A spell with delve can be paid for in part by exiling cards from your graveyard instead of paying generic mana.
destroy: To destroy a permanent moves it to its owner's graveyard as a state-based/effect result, distinct from sacrificing (destroy can be prevented by indestructible; sacrifice cannot).
devour: Devour N means as a creature with devour enters, it may sacrifice any number of other creatures, entering with N +1/+1 counters for each creature sacrificed.
discard: To discard a card moves it from a player's hand to their graveyard.
double strike: A creature with double strike deals combat damage in both the first-strike and regular combat damage steps.
double-faced card: A double-faced card has different characteristics on each of its two faces and can transform or be cast from either side depending on its type.
draw: To draw a card moves the top card of a player's library into their hand.
dredge: Instead of drawing a card, you may put a card with dredge from your graveyard into your hand by milling that many cards from your library.
echo: A permanent with echo requires you to pay its echo cost at the start of your first upkeep after it enters, or you sacrifice it.
embalm: By exiling this card from your graveyard and paying its embalm cost, you create a token copy of it (a Zombie, and colorless if it wasn't already).
emerge: A creature with emerge can be cast by paying its emerge cost and sacrificing a creature, reducing the generic mana cost by that creature's mana value.
entwine: A spell with entwine and multiple modes can be cast choosing all its modes by paying the entwine cost.
epic: A spell with epic can't be countered by spells and causes you to cast a copy of it, without paying its cost, at the beginning of each of your upkeeps for the rest of the game; you can't cast other spells for the rest of the game.
equipment: Equipment is an artifact subtype that can attach to creatures via its equip ability, granting a bonus while attached.
escalate: A spell with escalate and multiple modes lets you choose additional modes by paying its escalate cost once per extra mode.
escape: A card with escape can be cast from the graveyard by paying its escape cost and exiling a number of other cards from the graveyard.
eternalize: By exiling this card from your graveyard and paying its eternalize cost, you create a token copy of it as a 4/4 Zombie (and colorless if it wasn't already).
evoke: A creature with evoke may be cast for its evoke cost, and if you do, it's sacrificed when it enters.
exalted: Whenever a creature you control attacks alone, it gets +1/+1 until end of turn for each instance of exalted you control.
exile: To exile a card or permanent removes it from its current zone and puts it into the exile zone, a holding area outside the normal game zones.
exploit: A creature with exploit lets you sacrifice a creature (including itself) as it enters to trigger its exploit ability.
extort: Whenever you cast a spell, you may pay {W/B}; if you do, each opponent loses 1 life and you gain that much life.
fabricate: Fabricate N means as a creature with fabricate enters, put N +1/+1 counters on it, or create N 1/1 Servo tokens.
fear: A creature with fear can only be blocked by artifact creatures and black creatures.
fetchland: A fetchland is a land that can be sacrificed, usually paying 1 life, to search your library for a land with a stated basic land type and put it onto the battlefield.
fight: When two creatures fight, each deals damage equal to its power to the other.
first strike: A creature with first strike deals its combat damage before creatures without first strike.
flanking: When a creature without flanking blocks a creature with flanking, the blocker gets -1/-1 until end of turn.
flash: A card with flash can be cast any time you could cast an instant, not just during your main phase.
flashback: A card with flashback can be cast from your graveyard for its flashback cost, then exiled instead of going to the graveyard again.
flying: A creature with flying can only be blocked by creatures with flying or reach.
forecast: Forecast lets you activate an ability once during your upkeep by revealing the card with forecast from your hand.
fortify: A Fortification card with fortify is attached to a land you control by paying its fortify cost, boosting that land's abilities.
goad: A goaded creature must attack each combat if able, and its controller must have it attack a player other than you if possible.
gravestorm: When you cast a spell with gravestorm, copy it once for each permanent put into a graveyard from the battlefield this turn.
haste: A creature with haste can attack and use tap abilities the turn it comes under your control, ignoring summoning sickness.
haunt: A creature or spell with haunt, when it dies or resolves, exiles and 'haunts' a creature, triggering its ability again when that creature dies.
hexproof: A permanent or player with hexproof can't be the target of spells or abilities its opponents control.
hideaway: A permanent with hideaway lets you look at the top cards of your library when it enters and exile one face down, playable later under a stated condition.
horsemanship: A creature with horsemanship can only be blocked by other creatures with horsemanship.
improvise: A spell with improvise can be paid for in part by tapping untapped artifacts you control instead of mana.
indestructible: A permanent with indestructible can't be destroyed by damage or effects that say 'destroy'.
infect: Damage dealt by a source with infect is dealt to creatures as -1/-1 counters and to players as poison counters instead of normal damage/life loss.
intimidate: A creature with intimidate can only be blocked by artifact creatures and creatures that share a color with it.
investigate: Investigate creates a Clue artifact token with '{2}, Sacrifice this artifact: Draw a card.'
keyword ability: A keyword ability is a single word or short phrase (like flying or trample) that represents a fixed, predefined rules effect.
keyword action: A keyword action is a single word or short phrase (like scry or fight) that represents a predefined game action a spell or ability can instruct.
kicker: A spell with kicker may be cast for an additional cost; if you pay it, the spell gains extra effects described on the card.
landcycling: Cycling that lets you search your library for a land of a stated type instead of drawing.
landfall: Landfall abilities trigger whenever a land enters the battlefield under your control.
legendary rule: The legendary rule is a state-based action requiring a player who controls two or more legendary permanents with the same name to keep only one and put the rest into their owners' graveyards.
lifelink: Damage dealt by a source with lifelink also causes its controller to gain that much life.
living weapon: An Equipment with living weapon creates a 0/0 black Germ token and attaches to it when it enters.
loyalty ability: A loyalty ability is an activated ability of a planeswalker, costing loyalty counters to add/remove, usable only once per planeswalker per turn and only at sorcery speed.
madness: If you'd discard a card with madness, you may instead cast it for its madness cost, or exile it.
mana ability: A mana ability is an activated or triggered ability that could add mana as it resolves and doesn't target; it uses the stack differently and can be played even when a player couldn't otherwise get priority.
mana burn: An old, now-removed rule under which unspent mana in a player's pool caused them to lose that much life at the end of a step or phase.
mana value: A card's mana value is the total amount of mana in its mana cost, regardless of color (formerly called converted mana cost).
megamorph: Like morph, but turning the creature face up also puts a +1/+1 counter on it.
menace: A creature with menace can't be blocked except by two or more creatures.
mill: To mill N cards means to put the top N cards of a library into its owner's graveyard without looking at them.
miracle: A card with miracle may be cast for its miracle cost immediately after you draw it as the first card drawn in a turn.
modular: Modular N means a creature enters with N +1/+1 counters and, when it dies, puts those counters on target artifact creature.
morph: A creature with morph may be cast face down as a 2/2 colorless creature with no name or abilities for {3}, then turned face up any time for its morph cost.
mulligan: A mulligan lets a player shuffle their hand back into their library and draw a new hand (typically one card smaller under the London mulligan).
multikicker: A spell with multikicker may have its kicker cost paid any number of times.
mutate: A creature spell with mutate may instead merge with a non-Human creature you own, combining into one permanent that uses the base creature's stats/name but gains all abilities.
myriad: Whenever a creature with myriad attacks, for each opponent other than the defending player, create a nonattacking attack trigger copy of that creature attacking that opponent, then exile the copies.
ninjutsu: During combat, you may return an unblocked attacker you control to hand to put a creature with ninjutsu from hand onto the battlefield attacking in its place.
outlast: A creature with outlast can be activated (tap, no summoning sickness restriction) only as a sorcery to put a +1/+1 counter on itself; it doesn't untap normally that turn.
overload: A spell with overload can be cast for its overload cost to change 'target' to 'each' for that instance of the word on its text.
persist: When a creature with persist dies, if it had no -1/-1 counters on it, return it to the battlefield with a -1/-1 counter on it.
phasing: A permanent with phasing alternates between phased in and phased out (treated as if it doesn't exist) at the start of its controller's untap step.
planeswalker: A planeswalker is a permanent type representing an ally that enters with loyalty counters; players can activate one of its loyalty abilities once per turn, and it can be attacked like a player.
poisonous: Poisonous N means whenever a creature with poisonous deals combat damage to a player, that player gets N poison counters.
populate: Populate lets you create a token that's a copy of a token you control (typically your biggest creature token).
proliferate: Proliferate lets you choose any number of permanents/players with a counter and give each one more of each kind of counter it already has.
protection: Protection from a quality prevents a permanent from being damaged, enchanted/equipped, blocked, or targeted by anything with that quality (DEBT: damage, enchant/equip, block, target).
protection from color: Protection from a color is protection whose quality is that color, applying the DEBT rules (damage, enchant/equip, block, target) against sources of that color.
provoke: Whenever a creature with provoke attacks, you may have target creature an opponent controls untap and block it if able.
prowess: Whenever you cast a noncreature spell, a creature with prowess gets +1/+1 until end of turn.
rampage: Rampage N gives a creature +N/+N until end of turn for each creature blocking it beyond the first.
reach: A creature with reach can block creatures with flying.
rebound: If a spell with rebound resolves, instead of going to the graveyard it's exiled, and its owner may cast it again from exile at the beginning of their next upkeep for free.
reconfigure: An Equipment creature with reconfigure can attach to another creature (becoming just an Equipment) for its reconfigure cost, or unattach by becoming a creature again.
recover: When a permanent with recover would go to the graveyard, you may pay its recover cost to return this card from the graveyard to hand instead.
regenerate: Regenerating a creature sets up a replacement effect that, the next time it would be destroyed this turn, instead taps it, removes it from combat, and heals its damage.
reinforce: A card with reinforce may be discarded to put a stated number of +1/+1 counters on target creature by paying its reinforce cost.
renown: Renown N means the first time a creature with renown deals combat damage to a player, put N +1/+1 counters on it and it becomes renowned.
replicate: A spell with replicate can be copied once for each time you pay its replicate cost when casting it.
riot: A creature with riot enters with your choice of a +1/+1 counter or haste.
ripple: Ripple N means when you cast a spell with ripple, you may reveal the top N cards of your library and cast any revealed cards with the same name for free.
sacrifice: To sacrifice a permanent, its controller (never an opponent) puts it from the battlefield into its owner's graveyard; it can't be regenerated or prevented by indestructible.
saga: Saga is an enchantment subtype that gains a lore counter each of its controller's main phases, triggering a chapter ability for each counter reached.
scavenge: By exiling this card from your graveyard and paying its scavenge cost, put that many +1/+1 counters on target creature (sorcery speed only).
scry: Scry N lets you look at the top N cards of your library and put any number of them on the bottom and the rest back on top in any order.
shadow: A creature with shadow can only block or be blocked by other creatures with shadow.
shockland: A shockland is a dual land that enters untapped if its controller pays 2 life, otherwise it enters tapped.
shroud: A permanent or player with shroud can't be the target of any spells or abilities, including its controller's.
sideboard: A sideboard is a set of additional cards a player may swap into their deck between games of a match.
soulbond: A creature with soulbond may pair with another unpaired creature as either enters, and both gain a bonus while paired.
soulshift: Soulshift N means when a creature with soulshift dies, you may return target Spirit card with mana value N or less from your graveyard to your hand.
splice: A spell with splice onto a subtype lets you reveal it from hand while casting a spell of that subtype, adding its effects for its splice cost.
split second: While a spell with split second is on the stack, players can't cast spells or activate abilities that aren't mana abilities.
state-based action: State-based actions are automatic game rule checks (like a creature with 0 toughness dying) performed continuously without using the stack.
static ability: A static ability is a continuous effect that's simply true as long as its source is in the appropriate zone, with no activation or trigger.
storm: When you cast a spell with storm, copy it once for each other spell cast before it this turn.
summoning sickness: A creature has summoning sickness (can't attack or use tap/untap abilities) until it has been under its controller's control continuously since their most recent turn began.
surveil: Surveil N lets you look at the top N cards of your library, then put any number of them into your graveyard and the rest back on top in any order.
suspend: A card with suspend may be exiled with time counters by paying its suspend cost, removing a counter each upkeep, and being cast free when the last is removed.
tap: Tapping a permanent turns it sideways to show it has been used; many abilities cost 'tap' as part of their activation cost.
the stack: The stack is the zone where spells and abilities wait to resolve, resolving one at a time in last-in-first-out order.
token: A token is a marker (often a card-shaped placeholder) that represents a permanent not represented by an actual card, created by a spell or ability.
trample: A creature with trample can assign excess combat damage beyond what's needed to destroy its blockers to the defending player or planeswalker instead.
transfigure: By paying a creature's transfigure cost and sacrificing it, search your library for a creature card with the same mana value, put it onto the battlefield, then shuffle.
transmute: By paying a card's transmute cost and discarding it, search your library for a card with the same mana value, reveal it, and put it into your hand, then shuffle.
tribute: Tribute N means an opponent chooses when a creature with tribute enters: put N +1/+1 counters on it, or it triggers an ability if they don't.
triggered ability: A triggered ability automatically happens when its trigger condition is met, written as 'when/whenever/at ... , effect'.
typecycling: Cycling that lets you search your library for a card of a stated type instead of drawing.
undying: When a creature with undying dies, if it had no +1/+1 counters on it, return it to the battlefield with a +1/+1 counter on it.
unearth: A card with unearth can be returned from graveyard to battlefield by paying its unearth cost, gaining haste, then being exiled at end of turn or if it would leave the battlefield.
unleash: A creature with unleash may enter with a +1/+1 counter, but then it can't block as long as it has one.
untap: Untapping a permanent turns it back to its normal position, typically during its controller's untap step, so it can be used again.
vanishing: A permanent with vanishing enters with time counters and one is removed each upkeep; when the last is removed, sacrifice it.
vigilance: A creature with vigilance doesn't tap when it attacks.
ward: A permanent with ward causes any spell or ability an opponent uses to target it to be countered unless that opponent pays the ward cost.
wither: Damage dealt by a source with wither is dealt to creatures as -1/-1 counters instead of normal damage.
